content: The Self-Refine framework's iterative, self-feedback refinement pipeline can be grounded by multiple-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms to resolve a core unaddressed limitation of the original Self-Refine: the risk of getting stuck in local optimization loops caused by consistent single-perspective blind spots. By injecting inconsistent alternative solving perspectives from self-reflection methods like Self-Contrast into each Self-Refine iteration, the framework can avoid over-refining along biased initial outputs without requiring any external supervision or training data.
